_meta.description: Append-only expansion for timeline control covering records, card forms, highlighted fields, filters, sorting, quick create, notes, posts, custom connectors, and form-designer behavior.

mcq.q: What is the Timeline control in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
options: A Gantt chart showing the agent's scheduled activities for the day
A unified chronological feed of all activities, notes, and posts associated with a record
A visualisation of the case escalation path
A reporting widget showing case resolution trends over time
mcq.explanation: The Timeline control displays all activities (emails, calls, tasks), notes, and posts related to a record in reverse-chronological order. It gives agents a complete interaction history in one place on the case or contact form.
wrongExplanations: Agent schedules are managed in the scheduling board or calendar view — not the Timeline.
Escalation paths are defined in SLAs — the Timeline shows interaction history, not escalation flows.
Reporting on resolution trends is done in dashboards and reports — not the Timeline control.

msRef.label: Timeline overview

mcq.q: An administrator wants agents to quickly see the most critical information from an email in the Timeline without opening the full record. Which feature enables this?
options: Activity View
Card Form
Highlights Panel
Quick View Form
mcq.explanation: A Card Form defines what fields are shown as the preview/summary for an activity in the Timeline. By customising the card form, administrators control which fields are visible on the collapsed timeline entry without opening the full record.
wrongExplanations: Activity View is the full list view of activities — not the collapsed timeline preview.
The Highlights Panel shows pinned key fields on the record itself — not timeline entry previews.
Quick View Forms are embedded read-only views of related records on a form — not the timeline entry preview.

msRef.label: Configure card forms for Timeline

mcq.q: In the Timeline control configuration, what is the purpose of the Highlights section?
options: To pin specific activities to the top of the timeline
To display key fields from the parent record in a prominent summary strip above the timeline
To filter the timeline to show only flagged interactions
To highlight overdue SLA timers in red
mcq.explanation: The Highlights section in the Timeline shows a configurable strip of key fields from the record itself (e.g. Case Priority, Customer, Status) directly above the activity feed — giving agents instant context without scrolling the form.
wrongExplanations: Pinning individual activities to the top is a separate timeline feature — not the Highlights section.
Filtering for flagged interactions is done via timeline filters — Highlights is a form field summary, not a filter.
SLA timer indicators appear in the SLA KPI section — not in the Timeline Highlights.

msRef.label: Timeline highlights configuration

mcq.q: Which activity types can be displayed in the Timeline control by default?
options: Email and Phone Call only
Email, Phone Call, Task, Appointment, and Notes
Only custom activities
All standard activity types plus custom activities registered in the system
mcq.explanation: The Timeline can display all standard activity types (Email, Phone Call, Task, Appointment, Fax, Letter) plus any custom activities created in the system. Administrators can configure which types are shown or hidden.
wrongExplanations: Email and Phone Call only is far too restrictive — the Timeline supports all activity types.
Email, Phone Call, Task, Appointment, and Notes are included but this list is not exhaustive — custom activities are also supported.
Only custom activities is incorrect — standard activity types are displayed by default.

msRef.label: Configure activity types in Timeline

mcq.q: A company wants to prevent agents from deleting notes directly in the Timeline. Which configuration achieves this?
options: Remove the Delete privilege on the Note entity in the agent security role
Disable the Delete option in the Timeline control configuration settings
Both approaches together — removing the Delete privilege on the Note entity in the security role AND disabling Delete in the Timeline control configuration
Use a Business Rule to prevent note deletion
mcq.explanation: Preventing note deletion in the Timeline requires both: removing the Delete privilege on the Note entity in the security role (platform-level enforcement), and disabling Delete in the Timeline control configuration (UI-level enforcement). Either alone can be bypassed.
wrongExplanations: Security role alone prevents deletion via API but the Timeline UI delete button would still appear (and error). Best practice uses both.
Timeline config alone hides the button in the UI but does not prevent deletion via other means like the Notes grid or API.
Business Rules operate on form field logic — they cannot block delete operations.

msRef.label: Timeline control configuration

mcq.q: What does configuring Records in the Timeline control settings refer to?
options: Setting which case records appear in the timeline
Enabling non-activity entity records (like Knowledge Articles linked to a case) to appear in the timeline
Defining the sort order of timeline entries
Setting the maximum number of items shown in the timeline
mcq.explanation: In the Timeline configuration, Records settings allow administrators to include non-activity entity records in the timeline — for example, showing linked Knowledge Articles or custom entity records alongside activities and notes.
wrongExplanations: Case records are the parent — the Timeline shows child activities and records related to the case, not other case records.
Sort order is a separate Timeline configuration option — not the Records setting.
The maximum items shown is controlled by a separate load count setting — not the Records configuration.

msRef.label: Configure records in Timeline

mcq.q: An agent wants to write a private note in the Timeline that should not be visible to customers in the portal. What feature is used?
options: Sticky Note
Internal Note (Is Internal flag on Notes)
Private Activity
Hidden Comment
mcq.explanation: Notes in the Timeline have an Is Internal flag. When checked, the note is marked as internal and is not surfaced on the customer portal. This allows agents to add private annotations visible only to staff.
wrongExplanations: Sticky Note is not a standard Dynamics 365 feature.
Private Activity is not a standard concept in Dynamics 365 activities.
Hidden Comment is not a standard feature — the Is Internal flag on Notes is the correct mechanism.

msRef.label: Internal notes in Timeline

mcq.q: Which Timeline configuration option controls how many timeline items are initially loaded when an agent opens a case?
options: Page Size
Load Count
Record Limit
Timeline Depth
mcq.explanation: The Load Count setting in the Timeline configuration controls how many activity and note records are retrieved and displayed when the form first loads. Keeping this number low improves form load performance for cases with large interaction histories.
wrongExplanations: Page Size is a list view concept — not a Timeline-specific setting.
Record Limit is not the specific Timeline configuration term — it is called Load Count.
Timeline Depth is not a standard configuration setting name.

msRef.label: Timeline performance settings

mcq.q: Where do makers add or configure the Timeline component on a model-driven app form?
options: In app settings under Site map
In the form designer, from Components, by dragging the Timeline component onto a form section
In Power Automate by adding a Dataverse trigger
In table views by enabling the timeline column
mcq.explanation: Microsoft says makers open the table form in the form designer, select Components, and drag the Timeline component into a section on the form.
wrongExplanations: The site map doesn't host the timeline component configuration.
Power Automate is unrelated to placing the timeline on a form.
Timeline is a form component, not a table view column toggle.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

mcq.q: Which Timeline setting controls whether users can create records such as phone calls, tasks, or appointments directly from the timeline?
options: Record type filter
Quick create
Highlight fields
Card form sort
mcq.explanation: The Quick create setting controls whether users can create supported records directly from the timeline.
wrongExplanations: Filters control visibility, not direct creation behavior.
Highlighted fields affect display emphasis, not record creation.
Card form sorting is unrelated.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

mcq.q: What is the purpose of a card form in timeline configuration?
options: It defines the columns shown in Advanced Find
It controls how a record type appears visually as a timeline card
It replaces the main form for the table
It determines security role access to the table
mcq.explanation: Card forms are used to define how records are rendered visually as cards within the timeline.
wrongExplanations: Advanced Find view columns are unrelated to timeline card forms.
Card forms do not replace the main form.
Security access is not controlled by card forms.

msRef.label: Use the model-driven app main form and its components

mcq.q: What do highlighted fields do in the Timeline control?
options: They force a field to become required on save
They surface selected data points prominently on the timeline card for quick scanning
They encrypt sensitive data in the timeline
They limit which users can see the record
mcq.explanation: Highlighted fields are used to surface selected data points prominently on the card so users can scan important details quickly.
wrongExplanations: Highlighting is a display behavior, not a form-validation rule.
Timeline highlights do not encrypt data.
Visibility is controlled through security, not highlights.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

mcq.q: Which built-in content types can the Timeline control surface out of the box?
options: Only custom connector data
Notes, posts, and activities
Queues, SLAs, and entitlements
Dashboards and charts
mcq.explanation: Microsoft describes timeline as surfacing notes, posts, and activities such as emails, phone calls, appointments, and tasks.
wrongExplanations: Custom connector data is an extension scenario, not the out-of-box-only answer.
Queues, SLAs, and entitlements are not the out-of-box timeline content types described here.
Dashboards and charts are not timeline entries.

msRef.label: Timeline Overview for Users

mcq.q: If a maker wants a timeline to show external records from outside Dataverse, what Microsoft-supported extension approach is documented?
options: Enable Power BI embedding on the form
Use custom connectors with the TimelineWallControl component
Add a quick view form under the timeline
Create a business rule on the external table
mcq.explanation: Microsoft documents using custom connectors with the TimelineWallControl component to surface external data as timeline records.
wrongExplanations: Power BI embedding is unrelated to timeline record injection.
Quick view forms don't turn external data into timeline entries.
Business rules do not extend timeline with external records.

msRef.label: Use custom connectors with the timeline in Power Apps

mcq.q: What does the initial load setting on the Timeline control influence?
options: How many items are loaded when the timeline first opens
How many security roles can view timeline cards
How many forms can be linked to the table
How many queues can be shown on a case
mcq.explanation: The initial load configuration determines how many timeline items are loaded when the control first opens.
wrongExplanations: Security role counts are unrelated to this setting.
Linked forms are not controlled by initial load.
Queues shown on a case are unrelated.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

mcq.q: Which statement about timeline filtering is correct?
options: Timeline filters can be configured to let users narrow displayed record types
Timeline filters permanently delete hidden records from Dataverse
Timeline filters replace security roles
Timeline filters are available only for notes
mcq.explanation: Timeline configuration includes filter behavior so users can narrow the displayed content by record type or activity type.
wrongExplanations: Filtering affects display, not data deletion.
Filters do not replace the security model.
Filters are not restricted to notes only.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

mcq.q: Which statement best describes timeline sorting?
options: Timeline supports sorting content chronologically to help users review activity history
Timeline sorting changes the actual modified-on date of records
Timeline sorting is configured only in Power BI
Timeline sorting is available only for appointments
mcq.explanation: Timeline is intended to show information over time in a single stream, and sorting controls help users review chronology.
wrongExplanations: Sorting does not change stored record dates.
Power BI is unrelated to timeline sorting configuration.
Sorting is not limited to appointments.

msRef.label: Timeline Overview for Users

mcq.q: A maker wants users to open a record from the timeline and see a richer full-form experience than the card provides. Which statement is true?
options: Card forms are the only forms a timeline record can ever use
Timeline cards are compact display surfaces; opening the record uses the underlying table form experience
Timeline can display records only if the table has no main form
Timeline disables full record navigation
mcq.explanation: Timeline cards provide compact display, but records still open into their underlying forms when users navigate into them.
wrongExplanations: Card forms do not replace the record's normal form experience.
Tables still use main forms for record interaction.
Timeline does not disable full navigation.

msRef.label: Use the model-driven app main form and its components

mcq.q: Which form type is directly relevant when a timeline entry uses quick-create behavior for a table?
options: Card form only
Quick create form
Quick view form
Business process flow form
mcq.explanation: When timeline quick-create is used, it relies on the table's quick create form.
wrongExplanations: Card forms affect card display, not quick-create data entry.
Quick view forms are read-only display components.
Business process flow forms are unrelated.

msRef.label: Create or edit model-driven app quick create forms

mcq.q: Which statement about notes on the timeline is correct?
options: Notes are timeline content and can be configured for create/delete behavior
Notes can appear only through custom connectors
Notes are never part of timeline because they are not activities
Notes require unified routing to display
mcq.explanation: Notes are a supported timeline content type and can be configured in timeline behavior such as create/delete availability.
wrongExplanations: Notes are supported out of the box.
Notes are not activities, but timeline supports notes in addition to activities.
Unified routing is unrelated.

msRef.label: Timeline Overview for Users

dragdrop.instruction: Match each Timeline feature to what it configures.
zones: Controls what fields show in the collapsed timeline entry preview
Shows key record fields in a summary strip above the timeline
Controls performance by limiting initial items loaded
Hides notes from the customer portal
Adds non-activity entity records to the timeline feed

correctMapping.Card Form: Controls what fields show in the collapsed timeline entry preview
correctMapping.Highlights: Shows key record fields in a summary strip above the timeline
correctMapping.Load Count: Controls performance by limiting initial items loaded
correctMapping.Is Internal flag on Notes: Hides notes from the customer portal
correctMapping.Records setting: Adds non-activity entity records to the timeline feed

dragdrop.explanation: Card Forms customise the preview. Highlights show parent record fields. Load Count is a performance setting. Is Internal hides notes from portals. Records settings add non-activity entities to the timeline.

msRef.label: Timeline control configuration

dragdrop.instruction: Match each timeline concept to its correct purpose.
zones: Defines how a record appears visually on the timeline card
Surfaces selected values prominently for fast scanning
Lets users create supported records directly from the timeline
Controls how many items load when the timeline first opens
Extends timeline with external or nonstandard record sources

correctMapping.Card form: Defines how a record appears visually on the timeline card
correctMapping.Highlighted fields: Surfaces selected values prominently for fast scanning
correctMapping.Quick create: Lets users create supported records directly from the timeline
correctMapping.Initial load: Controls how many items load when the timeline first opens
correctMapping.Custom connector: Extends timeline with external or nonstandard record sources

dragdrop.explanation: These settings control how timeline content looks, what is emphasized, how records are created, how many items load initially, and how the timeline can be extended.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

dragdrop.instruction: Put the basic timeline setup process in the correct order.
items: Open the target form in the designer
Drag the Timeline component into a section
dragdrop.explanation: Microsoft's documented setup path starts with the table, then Forms, then the target form, then Components, and finally dragging the Timeline component into place.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

dragdrop.instruction: Classify each item as a timeline display concern or a broader platform/form concern.
items: Main form navigation after opening a record
dragdrop.explanation: Some settings are specific to the timeline surface itself, while others depend on the wider form and table model in model-driven apps.

msRef.label: Use the model-driven app main form and its components

casestudy.title: The Timeline and Agent Experience
casestudy.scenario: Fabrikam customer service team is experiencing issues with their case handling interface. Agents report the following problems: 1. The Timeline on case records takes 12 or more seconds to load because some cases have over 500 activities 2. Agents cannot tell the difference between a customer complaint email and a routine update email just from the Timeline — they have to open each one 3. A supervisor noticed agents are deleting notes from the Timeline that should be retained 4. Agents are adding notes visible to customers via the portal when they should be internal only 5. Product managers want linked Knowledge Articles to appear directly in the Timeline alongside activities The admin team must resolve all five issues.

msRef.label: Configure Timeline control

questions.q: The Timeline takes 12 or more seconds to load on cases with 500 or more activities. What is the most direct configuration fix?
options: Archive old activities to a separate table
Reduce the Timeline Load Count setting to retrieve fewer items on initial load
Upgrade to a higher Dynamics 365 tier with better performance
Split the Timeline into two separate sections — one for emails and one for other activities
questions.explanation: The Timeline Load Count setting controls how many records are fetched on initial load. Reducing it (e.g. to 10 or 20) dramatically improves load time — agents can load more records on demand if needed.
wrongExplanations: Archiving activities is a data management strategy — it does not fix the form load performance directly and requires significant data migration work.
Performance tier upgrades address compute capacity — the Timeline load issue is a configuration problem solvable without a tier change.
Splitting into two Timeline sections is not a standard feature — and would not address the underlying data volume fetch problem.

msRef.label: Timeline load count setting

questions.q: Agents need to distinguish email types from the collapsed Timeline view. Which configuration change achieves this?
options: Enable colour coding for email activities in the Timeline
Customise the Card Form for Email to display the Subject, From, and a key body field as the timeline preview
Add a Required flag to emails based on content
Enable the Timeline search bar so agents can filter by keyword
questions.explanation: Customising the Card Form for the Email activity controls what appears in the collapsed Timeline preview. Adding Subject, From, and a relevant field allows agents to identify the email type without opening it.
wrongExplanations: Colour coding is not a standard out-of-box Timeline configuration for differentiating email types.
Adding a Required flag does not change the visible information in the Timeline preview.
The search bar helps agents find specific records — it does not change what information is visible per activity in the Timeline.

msRef.label: Card forms in Timeline

questions.q: Agents are deleting notes from the Timeline. What is the most complete way to prevent this?
options: Remove the Delete privilege on the Note entity in the agent security role only
Disable the delete option in the Timeline configuration only
Both: remove Delete privilege on Note in the security role AND disable delete in the Timeline configuration
Enable audit logging on the Note entity to track deletions
questions.explanation: The most complete protection combines removing Delete privilege on the Note entity (platform-level) with disabling delete in the Timeline configuration (UI-level). Security role alone leaves the button visible causing errors; Timeline config alone can be bypassed via other access methods.
wrongExplanations: Security role alone prevents deletion via API but the Timeline delete option would still appear and show errors to agents.
Timeline configuration alone hides the UI button but does not prevent deletion through the full Notes grid or via API access.
Audit logging tracks deletions after the fact — it does not prevent them.

msRef.label: Timeline and note security

questions.q: Agents are adding notes visible to customers in the portal when they should be internal. How should agents mark notes as internal?
options: Prefix the note with [INTERNAL] in the text body
Set the Is Internal flag when creating a note in the Timeline
Add the note to a private queue instead of the case timeline
Change the note owner to the agent supervisor
questions.explanation: The Is Internal flag on a note, when checked, marks it as internal-only. The customer portal respects this flag and does not surface internal notes to customers — only staff can see them.
wrongExplanations: Prefixing with [INTERNAL] is a manual convention — the portal does not recognise it and would still display the note to customers.
Adding a note to a queue is not a standard feature — notes are attached to records, not queues.
Changing the note owner to a supervisor has no effect on portal visibility.

msRef.label: Internal notes in Timeline

questions.q: Product managers want linked Knowledge Articles to appear in the Timeline alongside activities. Which Timeline setting enables this?
options: Enable the Knowledge Article activity type in Timeline activity filters
Configure the Records setting in the Timeline to include the Knowledge Article entity
Add a subgrid for Knowledge Articles below the Timeline
Enable the Knowledge Base Search control on the case form
questions.explanation: The Timeline Records configuration allows non-activity entities — such as Knowledge Articles — to appear in the Timeline feed alongside standard activities and notes.
wrongExplanations: Knowledge Articles are not an activity type — they cannot be added via the activity type filter.
A subgrid shows related records in a separate table section — not integrated into the Timeline feed.
The Knowledge Base Search control enables agents to search for articles — it does not show linked articles in the Timeline.

msRef.label: Records in Timeline configuration

casestudy.title: Contoso Case Timeline Redesign
casestudy.scenario: Contoso wants service representatives to review customer history faster from the case form. They want key email and phone details emphasized on cards, supported activities created from the timeline, a manageable number of records loaded initially, and a way to show external records from another system inside the same timeline experience.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

questions.q: Which feature should Contoso use to emphasize the most important fields on each timeline card?
options: Highlighted fields
Business rules
Status reason transitions
Quick view form
questions.explanation: Highlighted fields are used to surface selected values prominently on the card.
wrongExplanations: Business rules don't control visual emphasis on timeline cards.
Status reason transitions are unrelated.
Quick view forms are different form components.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

questions.q: Contoso wants users to create calls and tasks directly from the timeline. What must be considered in addition to timeline settings?
options: The relevant quick create forms must support the scenario
The queue must be private
A Power Automate flow is required
The case must be resolved first
questions.explanation: Timeline quick-create relies on the relevant table quick create forms, so those forms must support the scenario.
wrongExplanations: Queue privacy is unrelated.
Power Automate is not required for basic timeline quick-create.
The case does not need to be resolved first.

msRef.label: Create or edit model-driven app quick create forms

questions.q: What setting should Contoso use to avoid overloading the form with too many timeline entries on first open?
options: Initial load count
Security role depth
Queue item threshold
Column requirement level
questions.explanation: Initial load controls how many timeline items are loaded when the control first opens.
wrongExplanations: Security role depth is unrelated.
Queue item thresholds are unrelated.
Requirement level is unrelated.

msRef.label: Add and configure the timeline control in Power Apps

questions.q: Contoso wants records from an external system in the same timeline stream. What Microsoft-supported path fits?
options: Custom connectors with TimelineWallControl
A dashboard chart pinned to the form
A quick view form only
Status reason transitions
questions.explanation: Microsoft documents custom connectors with TimelineWallControl for surfacing external data as timeline records.
wrongExplanations: Charts don't become timeline entries.
Quick view forms don't inject external rows into timeline.
Status reason transitions are unrelated.

msRef.label: Use custom connectors with the timeline in Power Apps

questions.q: What do card forms primarily affect in this scenario?
options: How timeline entries are visually rendered
Whether the case can be resolved
Whether SLAs pause
Whether the queue receives emails
questions.explanation: Card forms affect the visual rendering of timeline entries.
wrongExplanations: Case resolution is unrelated.
SLA pause behavior is unrelated.
Email queue receipt is unrelated.

msRef.label: Use the model-driven app main form and its components
